title: Stability results for Berge-matching in hypergraphs
abstract: Given a graph $F$, a hypergraph is called a Berge-$F$ if it can be obtained by expanding each edge of $F$ into a hyperedge containing it. Let $M_{k}$ denote the matching of size $k$. Kang, Ni, and Shan [12] determined the Turán number of Berge-$M_k$. Our main result shows that if an $r$-uniform hypergraph $H$ on $n$ vertices has nearly as many edges as the extremal in their theorem without containing $M_k$, then $H$ must be structurally close to certain well-specified graphs. Meanwhile, our result also implies several stability results, such as the stability version of the well-known Erdős-Gallai theorem (Erdős and Gallai, 1959 [5]).
